# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash -xve  # enables exit on error, print debug info (including executed commands), verbose mode for bash scripts to allow testing of each line before executing it (.v stands for verbosity)
# ensure that the AWS CLI is installed and configured with correct credentials set in ~/.aws/credentials. If not configure properly then this will fail when calling 'ecs-cli' command later on – use aws cli to manage your services directly through commands or GUI interface if required by ECS task definition
# Ensure that AWS CLI is correctly configured with access and secret keys (access key ID, Secret Access Key). These details can be set via the configure function in 'aws-cli'. If not properly installed/configured then when calling any aws command it fails. ‘AWS_ACCESS_KEY’ & 'AWSSecretAccessKey' must match these settings if you want to use AWS CLI for deploying your application
# Assuming that the script is being run in an environment where ecs task definition and role have been created previously with appropriate permissions, otherwise it would fail. ‘ECS_CLUSTER’ should be replaced by name of ECS cluster & 'FARGATE_SERVICE' must contain Fargate launch type as specified to deploy on aws fargated serverless application
# If not configured properly then the script will have issues in execution, it can only work within AWS CI/CD pipelines. ‘ECR’ and S3 bucket for storing Docker image etc need proper setup using ECS task definition file ecs-taskdef.json on aws s3 as well
# It's assumed that you already installed the necessary tools (aws cli, jq) with package manager of your OS in 'ecrget', and exported AWS credentials for CI/CD pipeline to run successfully . ‘AWS_ACCESSKEY’ & AWSSecretAccessKey should be set up as per above mentioned
# The script is designed only locally but it can also function on EC2 instances running IAM Roles. In this case, you would replace 'ECS' with Ec2 role name and configure your AWS CLI settings accordingly before executing the command ‘aws ecs register-container'' for deploying to aws fargated serverless application
AWS_ACCESSKEY=<Your Access key> AWSSecretAccessKey=<your Secret access Key>  ECRRepoURL=`echo ${ECR}/${MYREPO}:latest | sed 's/ /\n/g' | xargs aws ecr get-authorization-token --region <Your AWS Region>__
docker build -t myapp:1.0 . && \ docker tag  myapp:1.0 $ECRRepoURL:latest; \ echo $? > lastrunbuildstatus ;\ if [ "`cat lastrunbuildstatus `" != '0' ]; then ECS_CLUSTER=${MYFARGATE}; aws ecs register-container --cluster $ECSTASKDEFINITIONS cluster myapp:1.0; else echo ""; fi
